VALUES: Bicycles, Skates, Roller Blades and Skateboards
Disruptive Behavior, Unsolicited Advertising and Other Unscheduled Activities

<p>Table tent space can be reserved for the Bengal Lair and Brady Student Commons in order to advertise for a variety of events. To reserve the right to place Table Tents, submit a reservation request <a href="space-advertising.php" target="_blank">here</a>. Size and length of display are restricted. Any non-approved table tents will be removed and discarded at the discrescretion of the Unions.</p>

<p>Signs for designated posting areas in the Unions must be taken to the Information Desks for approval and posting.</p><ul><li>Only signs advertising upcoming events held on campus will be approved.</li><li>Events must be sponsored by student organizations or campus departments</li><li>Signs must be appropriate for display in a public environment and no larger than 11"x17".</li></ul><p>Once approved, the sign will be hung by a member of the Unions staff. Signs may be hung for a period of two weeks, and will be taken down following the day of the event. If the display board is full, your sign will be hung as soon as space permits.</p><p>Any postings in unauthorized areas will be removed and discarded.  Details regarding the advertising can be found here.  One public posting bulletin board is located with the Memorial Student Union and does not require approval.  This bulletin board is cleared often.</p>

<p>The possession of and/or discharge of firearms and/or explosives (including fireworks), other weapons or hazardous chemicals on Unions property is prohibited except by designated University agents or designated employees in the line of duty. Appropriate civil actions will be initiated for violators of this policy. See <a href="http://conduct.missouri.edu/wp-content/uploads/2013/08/M-Boom_Web_FIN.pdf">M-Book policy</a> for student conduct.</p>

<p>Any event hosted by a University department or recognized student organization may require University police department (MUPD) coverage. Any event after 11 PM and OVER 100 people, no matter the nature of the event, will require MUPD security coverage.  Security can only be arranged by MUPD. All MUPD reservations must be arranged no less than 10 (ten) business days before the event. Reservations are deemed as tentative until an MUPD representative/s has been contacted and a security confirmation is given. Notification of MUPD for events over 100 people and past 11 PM will be completed by Unions Administration. Fees for this security will be assessed and must be prepaid.  Cancellation of the event will forfeit this security fee.</p><p>Notification of the MUPD representative is coordinated by the Unions Administration. Based upon the type of event and past experience with similar events, MUPD will assign the appropriate number of staff to provide security to the function. Due to staff availability, large campus-wide events and previously scheduled activities will take precedence when scheduling security coverage.</p><p>It is the responsibility for the sponsoring organization/department, facility representative and MUPD representative(s) to promote and encourage a safe and secure environment for all participants.</p>

<p>When hazardous weather is imminent, the Missouri Student Unions will follow appropriate emergency action plans and campus protocol to meet the safety and security of the MU campus and community. We ask that all guests follow procedures and comply with instructions during these weather and safety situations.</p><p>When the University of Missouri issues a <span style="text-decoration: underline;">full closure</span> of the University of Missouri, the Missouri Student Unions will cancel all reservations within our facilities and not assess cancellation fees.  If the closure is related to cold weather/snow, the MU student Center will remain open 24 hours as a warming location to provide adequate services for the campus.  For announcements related to campus closure visit <a href="http://mualert.missouri.edu" target="_blank">MU Alert</a><a href="http://munews.missouri.edu/"></a>.</p><p>For more information regarding rain sites for reservations, please visit our <a href="http://www.unions.missouri.edu/space-rain-sites.php">policy</a>.</p>

<p>The Missouri Student Unions is supported through student fees to support the facilities, programming and services to the entire MU student population. Thus policies and parameters regarding building use are developed to create a productive and positive learning environment for MU students. We can and do limit activities, guests and use of space to reflect the needs of all MU Students.</p><p>University student organizations, individuals or departments may not serve as "fronts" for off-campus groups in order to gain unauthorized use of meeting space or information tables for the off-campus user. Meetings and events reserved by University student organizations or departments must fall under the following guidelines:</p><ul><li>The event/meeting must be conceptualized, planned, and managed by the University student organization or department and must truly be an organization or department initiative.</li><li>Any costs associated with the event/meeting must be paid by the student organization either by providing a University account number or from the checking account belonging to the student organization.</li><li>If The Unions suspects and/or believes that a reservation is fronting, reservation capabilities for that sponsoring organization will be revoked.</li></ul><p>To be good stewards of our resources, The Unions promotes and supports the initiatives and programs of the University, departments and student organizations, not individuals or businesses. The Unions will make the determination as to on-campus vs. off-campus sponsorship by considering the nature of the event, past experience with respect to similar events, type, and the nature of attendees.</p>

<p>Any person who engages in misconduct in the Missouri Student Unions may be requested to leave the premises. In the event that any person(s) engaged in misconduct refuses to leave the premises MUPD will be called to assist.</p><p>Misconduct shall be defined as behavior which in any way defaces or damages the premises, or harassing of customers and guests or obstructs or interferes with the intended use of the premises. Guests are asked to be courteous to fellow guests and staff while patronizing Unions facilities. Abusive language and behavior will not be tolerated and guests will be asked to leave the premises. Federal and state law, city ordinances and University policies apply.</p><p>Guests are also asked to wear a shirt while being in the Unions facilities</p>

<p>Due to high campus demand for meeting space and information tables, customers who fail to cancel their reserved space or do not show up will be charged a cancellation fee. All users have 48 hours prior to a scheduled meeting/event to cancel otherwise, a cancellation fee per room, plus any room charges, equipment, miscellaneous charges and room reset fees will be billed to the customer.</p><p>Cancellation fees for meeting rooms and information tables are $30 and $50 for ballrooms/large rooms. Users are not allowed to use the reserved space for functions other than originally reserved and rooms will be periodically checked for occupancy. An individual who occupies a room in an effort to circumvent a cancellation fee will still incur the cancellation fee.   Failure to meet these requirements will also result in a cancellation fee being assessed. Failure to pay cancellation fees will result in denial of future reservations until paid.</p><p>The Unions reserves the right to change and/or cancel a reservation or alter the use of other assigned space with the understanding that, at all times, every effort shall be made to provide comparable facilities.</p><p>If the MU campus is in "full closure" reservations will be cancelled and will not incur any fees.  See <a href="http://unions.missouri.edu/space-weather">weather section</a> for more information. </p>

<p>Charitable organizations will be allowed to use available tables at no charge.  The Unions reserves the right to deny use of tables and Unions facilities to charitable organizations who compete with interests within the Missouri Student Unions. (see policy) The Unions reserves the right to limit charitable organizations use of information tables.  </p><p>Charitable organizations are welcome to utilize meeting space in our facilities for a fee.</p><p>The Unions requires that there is a representative of the organization at the event at all times and that participants only occupy the space allocated. If any type of damage occurs during these events, organizations will be assessed a damage fee in accordance to the extent of the damage incurred. The Unions reserves the right to deny use of facilities if the event conflicts with University policy and/or any services offered within the facility.</p>

<ul><li>The sponsoring organization/department of an event hosted within the Unions is responsible for both the safety of persons attending and for returning the rooms back to their original condition. If rooms are not returned to their original condition, a room reset fee will be assessed.</li><li>Attendance at all events is limited to the number of fixed seats in the rooms or established capacities of rooms as dictated by fire and safety codes. Standing in the aisles and sitting on the back of seats during any program is prohibited except for persons working the event.</li><li>All materials used for decorations must be fire retardant or flame proof in accordance with University regulations.</li><li>Exits must open readily and be accessible at all times. During the period of use, no required exit door may be fastened so that the door cannot be opened readily from the inside by use of ordinary doorknob or by pressure on the crash bar. Exit ways serving the room must be adequately lighted during all times that the room is occupied.</li><li>Should occupancy reach fire code or deemed excessive by building management, customers will be asked to reduce attendance. Should customers refuse, MUPD will be called to assist and reservation capabilities will be jeopardized.</li></ul>

<p>All students who are holding any type of fundraising event in the Memorial Student Union or MU Student Center must follow this procedure:</p><ul><li>Make the reservation(s) first. Please indicate during the request that this event will be for fundraising purposes. This enables organizers to ensure that the space and date(s) they desire is available. This reservation will be tentative pending approval from The Organizational Resource Group.</li><li>Go to <a href="http://www.getinvolved.missouri.edu/" target="_blank">getinvolved.missouri.edu</a> to fill out the appropriate fundraising paperwork.</li><li>All approved fundraising paperwork should be submitted at least one week prior to the event to the Event Management Office. At this time we will change your reservation to “confirmed.”</li><li>Retain a copy of the approval form for group records and leave another copy of that form with the Event Management office.</li><li>This procedure will need to be followed for each set of fundraising events.</li><li>Please refer to the <a href="http://mizzoulife.missouri.edu/resources/m-book/" target="_blank">M-Book policies</a> regarding fundraisers for your reference.  All policies must be followed. </li><li>The Unions reserves the right to deny access to any group promoting products or services that are in direct competition with current interests within the Union buildings. The Unions also retains the right to deny access to a group based upon the needs and/or best interests of the University of Missouri.</li><li>Please refer to the <a href="http://mizzoulife.missouri.edu/resources/m-book/" target="_blank">M-Book policies</a> regarding fundraisers for your reference. All policies must be followed.</li></ul>

<p>If groups damage, harm or abuse spaces within or around the MU Student Center and Memorial Student Union, the Missouri Student Unions reserves the right to revoke reservation and events capabilities for at least six months from the date of revoked services and put them on notice. In addition, all future reservations will be cancelled. After six months, the group may request to have this status removed and capabilities restored, however, all overdue fees must also be paid to begin to utilize the Missouri Student Unions.</p>

<p>The Missouri Student Unions reserves the right to deny access to any group promoting products or services that are in direct competition with current interests within the Union buildings. The Unions also retains the right to deny access to a group based upon the needs and/or best interests of the University of Missouri.</p>

<p>The Missouri Student Unions follows M-Book policy regarding films, screenings and other copyrighted materials.  Student organizations and University departments may not show or exhibit copyrighted materials on campus without first receiving the appropriate rights and permissions from the MSA/GPC Films committee.  All reservations which denote a screening, film or other copyrighted material will remain tentative untill approval from the MSA/GPC Films Committee has been granted.</p><p>Please refer to the comprehensive policy in the <a href="http://mizzoulife.missouri.edu/resources/m-book/" target="_blank">M-Book</a>.</p>

<p>Requests to place memorials in the Unions or under the Tower should be directed to the Unions Administration, and are for no longer than 72 hours. Memorials will not be approved if they impede the use of the facility or pose a safety risk. Any memorials not collected by their owner after 72 hours become the property of the Unions.</p>

<p>The manufacture, sale, distribution, or possession, or use of narcotic, hallucinogenic, hypnotic, depressant, and/or stimulating drugs by any users of the Missouri Student Unions buildings without proper prescription or required license is prohibited. Appropriate civil action will be initiated for violators of this policy.</p>
Memorial Union, University of Missouri Columbia, MO 65201
Associated Students of the University of Missouri
